codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers the feature requirements. Created metrics.rs module with MetricsWindow enum for time aggregation (Hour/Day/Week/AllTime), MetricSample struct for individual samples, AggregatedMetrics for weighted averages, AlertThresholds with configurable limits (min_completion_rate: 0.7, max_retry_rate: 0.5, max_cost_per_task: 500k tokens, min_agent_utilization: 0.3, max_validation_failure_rate: 0.3), MetricsAlert with AlertType and AlertSeverity, MetricsCollector with circular buffer (60 samples), and OrchestratorMetrics for computing current state from orchestrator data. All 5 required metric types are tracked: completion rate, validation pass rate, retry rate, cost/task, and agent utilization. Time aggregation and alert thresholds are implemented as specified. 11 tests cover the functionality.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed documented Rust conventions properly - used async_trait, thiserror patterns, proper module structure with lib.rs exports. No convention violations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker created metrics.rs with proper public API exports via lib.rs

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the Rust skill's guidance appropriately. Used chrono for datetime, serde for serialization, tokio::sync::RwLock for thread-safe access. skillFeedback confirms followedProcedure: true with no deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker correctly used chrono::{DateTime, Duration, Utc}, serde::{Deserialize, Serialize}, tokio::sync::RwLock

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used standard cargo commands for build and test as documented in services.yaml. No unexpected services or commands.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: cargo build --workspace, cargo test -p swell-orchestrator -- metrics

summary: Review passed. The observability-metrics feature is fully implemented with all required metric types (completion rate, validation pass rate, retry rate, cost/task, agent utilization), time aggregation via MetricsWindow enum, and configurable alert thresholds via AlertThresholds struct. 11 tests provide good coverage. Build and tests pass. No blocking issues found. Worker followed proper conventions and procedure.
